115427.explanation: This often creates expectations. It is only possible if it is very clear beforehand what will be done with the outcome.

115429.explanation: Allowing side activities at farms should depend on spatial, environmental, and social considerations. Not every location or activity is suitable or safe within the zoning plan and living environment.

115431.explanation: Distribution centers can provide employment and economic growth and strengthen the municipality’s regional logistical role. Attention is needed for additional pressure on space, extra traffic, and potential landscape impact.

115432.explanation: Asylum reception is a social and legal responsibility and contributes to solidarity and humane shelter for people in need of protection.

115433.explanation: The distribution law is in effect and requires a fair contribution from all municipalities. By establishing a reception center, the municipality fulfills its legal responsibility and contributes to balanced and humane asylum seeker reception.

115435.explanation: Replanting for every tree felled is necessary to maintain greenery, biodiversity, and quality of life and contributes to a sustainable and future-proof municipality.

115436.explanation: Fireplaces and wood stoves contribute to atmosphere and traditional home comfort and, when used responsibly, can supplement sustainable heating options.

115438.explanation: A municipal fireworks show reduces nuisance and danger from private fireworks, provides entertainment for residents, and is easier to control and safer.

115442.explanation: Housing development on agricultural land fragments the countryside and puts pressure on landscape, nature, and spatial planning.

115443.explanation: Paying for sports memberships promotes health, social participation, and increases chances of inclusion in society. On the other hand, it is an additional structural expense, and sports participation might also be encouraged through existing schemes or personal responsibility.

115444.explanation: Extra municipal support may influence the independence of the local broadcaster, and resources could be better used for essential municipal tasks.

115447.explanation: Sports clubs should primarily operate independently, and financial support from the municipality is only needed in exceptional cases or for social projects.

115449.explanation: More enforcement officers increase visible presence and safety, can address nuisance faster, and contribute to a feeling of safety among residents.

115450.explanation: More camera surveillance can reduce crime and nuisance and increase safety in public spaces. However, too many cameras can infringe on privacy, involve high costs, and do not guarantee a structural reduction of nuisance or crime. Tailored solutions are essential!

115451.explanation: Setting up and maintaining such a facility requires significant resources; collaborating with existing regional services can be more efficient and effective.

115452.explanation: A starter’s loan helps young people or those with limited income to buy their first home, promotes mobility in the housing market, and makes living in the municipality more accessible.

115453.explanation: Priority for residents stimulates social cohesion, shortens waiting times for local home seekers, and ensures municipal housing primarily benefits the local community.

115455.explanation: Allowing permanent residence in holiday homes can increase housing supply and ensure vacant buildings are put to good use. However, holiday homes are not suitable as permanent residences; permanent use can lead to problems with infrastructure, facilities, and zoning plans.

115457.explanation: Social work provides structure for benefit recipients, increases social participation, contributes to the community, and helps develop skills and work experience.

115461.explanation: Designating more parking spaces as charging points can create scarcity for regular parking places and can be costly to install and maintain.

115462.explanation: A 30 km/h speed limit within built-up areas increases traffic safety for pedestrians and cyclists. Too low speeds can hinder traffic flow and lead to more violations. Thirty where necessary, fifty where possible.

115463.explanation: Freedom of choice for healthcare providers aligned with faith, culture, or belief increases trust, care quality, and client satisfaction but may lead to higher costs or limited availability.

115465.explanation: Property tax (ozb) is an important source of municipal income; a reduction can lead to less funding for amenities and maintenance of the living environment.

115467.explanation: Higher tourist tax provides additional income for the municipality, which can be used for maintenance of public spaces, tourist facilities, and livability.

115473.explanation: School swimming is the responsibility of parents and schools themselves; the municipality can better use its resources for other broad social services.

115476.explanation: High residential towers disrupt the streetscape, can affect the living environment, and do not always fit the scale and atmosphere of Heemskerk.

115478.explanation: It is not always efficient or necessary to have a neighborhood center in every district; existing centers or multifunctional facilities can often fulfill the same functions at lower costs.

115479.explanation: Heemskerk does not have paid parking in the town center.

115480.explanation: More parking spaces in the center encourage car traffic, take away space for greenery and pedestrians, and actually discourage sustainable transport.

115482.explanation: Smoke-free playgrounds protect children from harmful tobacco smoke, promote healthy behavior, and ensure a safer and cleaner play environment.

115427.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA considers participation very important. Therefore, we advocate for involvement of residents in specific projects that concern them, sounding boards, citizen initiatives, and conducting surveys. In our view, a referendum provides too little information about why people hold certain opinions.

115429.explanation: Agricultural areas must remain agricultural. We do support small-scale, non-commercial projects.

115431.explanation: Distribution centers have no place in Heemskerk. They cause nuisance and do not contribute to the economy of tomorrow.

115432.explanation: There is currently no asylum seeker center in Heemskerk.

115433.explanation: Recently, the municipal council together with Uitgeest decided that an asylum seeker center will be established. This complies with the distribution law. GroenLinks-PvdA believes every municipality has a duty to arrange good accommodation for refugees.

115435.explanation: Heemskerk is a green municipality, and we want to keep it that way. Therefore, for every tree cut down, we plant two back.

115436.explanation: Wood burning (wood stoves and fire pits) causes nuisance and demonstrable health damage. We want this to stop in Heemskerk. We aim for a ban by focusing on awareness, discouragement, and adjustments such as fine particle filters.

115438.explanation: We are against setting off fireworks. It is harmful to air quality and causes nuisance for people and animals. Moreover, it causes health damage. However, we support alternatives such as light shows.

115442.explanation: This should only be allowed as an exception. Agricultural land remains agricultural land in principle.

115443.explanation: Sports are healthy. Everyone must be able to participate in sports, even if you don't have much money. We want to investigate if this can be done through the Adults' Sports & Culture Fund (this fund already exists for youth).

115444.explanation: Now that a merger is imminent between the different broadcasters, supporting the local broadcaster well is good for democracy.

115447.explanation: Sports are healthy and a club is an important place to combat loneliness. Therefore, it is important to financially support these clubs if they are struggling.

115449.explanation: More enforcement can be a solution in some cases, but only if it contributes to solving a concrete problem. We prefer to focus on prevention so that enforcement is not necessary.

115450.explanation: Camera surveillance can be a solution in exceptional cases, but everyone also has a right to privacy. Therefore, we are cautious about using camera surveillance.

115451.explanation: It is necessary that victims of domestic violence have a safe place to go to.

115452.explanation: This way, starters also get the chance to enter the housing market.

115453.explanation: At present, there is room to offer 25% of the supply to people with social or economic ties. This must remain so but should not increase.

115455.explanation: These homes often do not meet quality standards, but if people live there out of necessity (for example, due to high debts), we should not evict them.

115457.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA is against a mandatory counter-performance. We encourage people to stay involved, but this must always be voluntary. We do support guidance towards work or participation in other ways.

115461.explanation: A good start has already been made here. It fits within our sustainability strategy to continue this. We will look at where there is room in the power network and in which neighborhoods more supply is needed.

115462.explanation: In principle, we assume 30 km/h zones. There are exceptions, for example, large asphalt roads between districts. 30 km/h is better for air quality and traffic safety.

115463.explanation: Everyone must feel comfortable with their healthcare provider. Religion, culture, or beliefs can play a role in this.

115465.explanation: The property tax (ozb) has been aligned with the national average. This way we can continue to perform municipal tasks so that the level of services is maintained.

115467.explanation: This is not a must; the tax must be high enough to cover costs. If necessary, it must therefore also be able to increase.

115473.explanation: There is no school swimming in Heemskerk.

115476.explanation: It is difficult to build houses in rural areas. We want more homes, so they must be built upwards, while keeping an eye on the surroundings.

115478.explanation: There is currently a good distribution of community centers in Heemskerk. It is important that this remains so.

115479.explanation: There is no paid parking in the center and there should not be any.

115480.explanation: There must remain parking spaces for people with mobility impairments, but the number of parking spaces does not need to grow.

115482.explanation: The new generation must grow up smoke-free.

115427.explanation: D66 wants residents to be involved as much as possible in policy-making. This can be through participation, consultation moments, or visiting the political group. However, we do not consider a local referendum a good tool.

115429.explanation: D66 is open to the sale of products to businesses. For example, in the Heemskerk horticultural area, you can buy beautiful bouquets everywhere. A small shop is therefore fine. A campsite must fit in the environment due to accessibility. There is no absolute 'always' on this.

115431.explanation: There is no place for large-scale distribution centers in Heemskerk. We will reject applications for such businesses.

115432.explanation: At the moment we have no asylum accommodation but establishing an asylum seeker center is on the agenda. We believe Heemskerk should do its part in the national task. We will implement this together with our residents and expect, as before, sufficient support.

115433.explanation: D66 Heemskerk believes Heemskerk must do its part in the national asylum accommodation task. We will realize an asylum seeker center together with our residents and, as before, expect to be able to create sufficient support. Safety and good integration are important starting points.

115435.explanation: D66 Heemskerk wants more greenery in our beautiful village. Good for combating heat stress, water retention, and providing space for sports and recreation. If a tree is removed, at least one new one must be planted. Wherever possible, we add more trees.

115436.explanation: Wood burning has significant effects on the environment and health. Therefore, D66 Heemskerk does not support it.

115438.explanation: D66 Heemskerk believes that once the national fireworks ban is in place, the municipality should organize a central fireworks show so our residents can end the year in a beautiful way.

115442.explanation: D66 Heemskerk supports the possibility of adding a house or a 'kangaroo house' on one's own land. However, this must comply with the rules regarding the horticultural concentration area of the Province.

115443.explanation: Through the Youth Fund for Sport & Culture, the municipality already pays for sports club memberships for children whose parents cannot afford it. D66 Heemskerk wants to expand this scheme to include participation in the Adult Fund. Sport should be accessible to everyone.

115444.explanation: We have a broadcaster in Heemskerk that is part of a regional cooperation. When necessary, we as a municipality can support this, but always in consultation with the other participating municipalities.

115447.explanation: During corona times, the municipality provided financial support to sports clubs. D66 Heemskerk believes the municipality can financially support a club for a short period in specific cases, provided there is an improvement plan to become self-sufficient again within a foreseeable time.

115449.explanation: Safety on the streets is an important issue for D66 Heemskerk. Ensuring and increasing safety in specific places is not free. Therefore, we want to invest in this if necessary.

115450.explanation: In specific places and during specific periods, such as New Year's Eve, camera surveillance can be a useful tool. It must always serve a purpose and be used temporarily. It is not a miracle solution and also infringes on the privacy of our residents.

115451.explanation: There must be a women's shelter in the region. This does not necessarily have to be in Heemskerk.

115452.explanation: We do have a starter loan as a municipality, but D66 Heemskerk considers it a flawed instrument because it also has a price-increasing effect. We prefer to build many more homes for specific groups such as starters and the elderly. That is more effective.

115453.explanation: We have a regional housing market and must also view it as such. We can allocate part of the social rental homes to local residents. D66 Heemskerk thinks allocating new construction to local residents is a good idea.

115455.explanation: D66 Heemskerk realizes that housing shortages sometimes require creative solutions but believes this is not a good idea.

115457.explanation: D66 Heemskerk strongly supports people on social assistance doing volunteer work to stay close to work and society. However, we do not support making this mandatory.

115461.explanation: The municipality must facilitate electric charging. If this is not possible on private property, we as a municipality should designate places and install charging stations where necessary.

115462.explanation: The maximum speed must fit the road design. There are places where we can reduce the speed from 50 to 30 km/h, but this is certainly not desirable everywhere. We will examine this per area because we do see the benefits of lower speeds in increasing safety.

115463.explanation: Basically agreed, but in practice this is not always possible and also not a direct responsibility of the municipality.

115465.explanation: The property tax (OZB) is not a cash cow but a means for the municipality to generate extra income to maintain a desired level of facilities. Unfortunately, funding from the national government is insufficient to do this at an acceptable level. The OZB should be as low as possible, as high as necessary.

115467.explanation: Part of the proceeds from the tourist tax goes towards promoting tourism in our village. The tourist tax is not a cash cow. We compare our rates with municipalities in the area and will certainly not exceed them.

115473.explanation: We currently do not do that and D66 Heemskerk does not intend to start. However, we do allocate funds via the Youth Sport and Culture Fund for families who are financially struggling. We do intend to organize a sea safety awareness program in schools.

115476.explanation: The housing shortage and limited space justify a reassessment when it comes to high-rise buildings. Of course, high-rise must take place in smart locations in good harmony with the surroundings.

115478.explanation: D66 believes there should be a good distribution of community centers in Heemskerk. This does not automatically mean that every neighborhood must have its own community center.

115479.explanation: There is no paid parking in the municipality of Heemskerk and D66 Heemskerk wants to keep it that way.

115480.explanation: D66 wants to focus on improving the recreational function of the center. More parking spaces do not fit into this. We aim for sufficient parking at the outer edges of the center.

115482.explanation: Our children should grow up as smoke-free as possible. A smoke-free playground and sports club belong to this.

115427.explanation: A referendum is often too black-and-white for complex dossiers. We prefer to involve residents early on in plans per neighborhood through active participation.

115429.explanation: We want to preserve the farmers in our rural area. Fewer local rules make this type of entrepreneurship and healthy business operations easier.

115431.explanation: We need entrepreneurs for a strong economy and jobs. However, we look critically at the scarce space; housing construction often has priority.

115432.explanation: The VVD Heemskerk is very critical of large-scale shelters. Our priority lies with the safety of our residents and space for our own housing construction.

115433.explanation: We recently submitted a motion to pause. We prefer to build for our own youth and starters.

115435.explanation: A green living environment is essential and we want to preserve it.

115436.explanation: The VVD stands for individual freedom without unnecessary bans. Residents choose themselves how to heat their homes. We rely on personal responsibility to avoid burning during a burn alert or unfavorable weather, taking air quality and neighbors into account.

115438.explanation: A municipal show costs a lot of taxpayers' money. VVD prefers to spend this money on street safety or lowering local taxes for our residents instead of a one-time event.

115442.explanation: The VVD wants to maintain the agricultural zoning where it currently applies to protect our rural area. Therefore, we are against regular housing construction on farmland. However, we do want to explore whether exceptions are possible in the future for situations like care homes on private property.

115443.explanation: Sport is important but not a free right at the expense of taxpayers. We expect personal initiative and a reciprocal contribution to society.

115444.explanation: A broadcaster should strive to be self-sufficient. The municipality should remain small and agile and focus on its statutory core tasks.

115447.explanation: We charge fair rents for sports fields, but clubs should basically be financially healthy and independent themselves.

115449.explanation: Safety is a top priority. More police presence on the streets and visible enforcement are needed to effectively tackle nuisance and crime.

115450.explanation: We use all necessary means to ensure safety in our neighborhoods and the center and to identify offenders faster. VVD emphasizes that camera surveillance is always a temporary instrument aimed at addressing specific hotspots and restoring order.

115451.explanation: This kind of specialized care should be organized regionally for the best quality and efficiency, not separately per small municipality.

115452.explanation: We are happy to help our young people get their first own home. A starter loan is a proven tool to get the housing market moving again.

115453.explanation: Our own residents come first. We make strict agreements with housing corporations so that Heemskerk residents are not at the back of the queue.

115455.explanation: Recreational areas must remain recreational. Permanent residence harms the natural values and the tourist appeal of our village.

115457.explanation: Rights and duties go hand in hand. Those receiving benefits contribute to the Heemskerk community according to their ability.

115461.explanation: The market should arrange this based on demand. We will not just sacrifice scarce regular parking spaces for a specific group.

115462.explanation: Safety in neighborhoods is paramount, but on through roads, 50 km/h is necessary for good traffic flow and accessibility of our village.

115463.explanation: Freedom of choice is a liberal core principle; religion, culture, or beliefs should not play a role in offering and accepting care. Good care must be equal and neutral for everyone.

115465.explanation: The recent 35% increase by the current administration is unacceptable and hits the wallets of Heemskerk residents hard. We want to lower the property tax to make living and doing business in our village affordable again.

115467.explanation: Tourists use the facilities and public spaces in Heemskerk. VVD Heemskerk considers an increase in the tourist tax a fair way for visitors to contribute to maintaining our village. This allows general municipal taxes for our own residents to stay lower.

115473.explanation: Swimming lessons are primarily the responsibility of parents, not the taxpayer. The municipality should provide a good and accessible swimming pool in the region, but the lessons are a private matter.

115476.explanation: We must be honest: to offer everyone a home, we must build upwards in suitable locations. This way we save our greenery. We do this in moderation and only where it fits, so the unique village character of Heemskerk is always preserved.

115478.explanation: This is unaffordable and unnecessary. We must use community money efficiently and encourage meeting at existing places.

115479.explanation: The VVD wants to retain free parking (blue zone). This is crucial to keep our center attractive for shoppers and entrepreneurs.

115480.explanation: An accessible center is a vital center. Visitors must be able to easily park their cars to visit our local shops.

115482.explanation: Children must be able to play safely and healthily. A smoke-free environment is a small effort with a large positive impact on health.

115427.explanation: A referendum is a very difficult instrument to handle and is actually not usable in local politics. Residents choose their political representatives who stand up for everyone's interests for four years. Of course, incumbent politicians must continue to listen to the residents.

115429.explanation: There are situations where that is undesirable – that is why we have adopted zoning plans. So it is not always possible. Only in special cases can an informed deviation from zoning plans occur.

115431.explanation: There is little space in Heemskerk, but a complete rejection of any economic activity is unwise. Good plans should have a chance.

115433.explanation: It is a legal and moral responsibility of municipalities to accommodate vulnerable refugees. It is necessary to create the right conditions, especially regarding safety.

115435.explanation: We must be careful with the scarce greenery we have.

115436.explanation: Research shows that there are significant health risks associated.

115438.explanation: Many people always enjoyed beautiful fireworks. Mainly for safety reasons, this is no longer allowed in the same way. Moreover, there are sustainable alternatives to traditional fireworks.

115442.explanation: Unclear statement: does this mean that zoning plans should standardly make this possible under all circumstances?

115443.explanation: CDA Heemskerk believes everyone should be able to participate in sports or cultural activities (such as music lessons).

115444.explanation: Local journalism is extremely important and the municipality must ensure it is not lost. Possibly with extra funding, but that is not always the right solution.

115447.explanation: Also for this statement, more context is important: sports clubs play an indispensable role in society – that must be secured. Money is not necessarily the solution to problems. Collaborating with other clubs, more focus on core tasks, etc., are often more effective.

115449.explanation: Neighborhood enforcement officers have proven their value to society. Heemskerk expanded capacity this year to 8 officers. In practice, it must now be seen if this is sufficient.

115450.explanation: Camera surveillance is an intrusive measure that infringes on residents' privacy and should therefore be applied cautiously. However, in cases of repeated local nuisance, camera surveillance can be very valuable to protect residents' living environment.

115451.explanation: Not against, but what problem does it solve? The biggest problem for the capacity of women's shelters in the IJmond region is lack of flow due to the severe housing shortage. Improving flow is more effective.

115452.explanation: Financial support to buyers drives up prices, meaning no more starters (or other target groups) find a home. Such a measure mainly benefits sellers.

115453.explanation: But the current South Kennemerland and IJmond region is too large. Bundling forces 'above the canal' (Beverwijk, Uitgeest, Castricum and BUCH) makes more sense.

115455.explanation: There are many reasons why this is generally not a good idea. For example, from an economic standpoint (tourism). Holiday homes are often not suitable for permanent residence. Permanent residence also puts pressure on facilities, etc. Tailored solutions are necessary.

115457.explanation: Not everyone can be asked this. A single parent with children has hands full with the parenting task, to name one example.

115461.explanation: With technological innovations (especially fast chargers and home charging), it is unnecessary to burden public space heavily with specific charging stations.

115462.explanation: Traffic safety will certainly improve with this. Exceptionally, clear-through roads can be excluded (currently parts of the outer ring and parts of the access roads from outside the municipality).

115463.explanation: I assume this means it is possible from an insurance perspective. Be careful about potential monopolies/cartels if there are few providers.

115465.explanation: The unchecked increases by the current left-wing coalition must be corrected.

115467.explanation: We as the council also chose this. A differentiated rate would be better though. (For example, there is currently no distinction between a hotel and a campsite.)

115473.explanation: Swimming is a life-saving skill. Everyone should be able to swim; preferably already in primary school age.

115476.explanation: This is an effective way to address the housing crisis.

115478.explanation: Community facilities (fortunately) do not stop at neighborhood boundaries. In a larger area, functions can also be combined. And the space does not have to be available in just a single neighborhood.

115480.explanation: More parking space in the center compromises the atmosphere (cozy, small-scale, ...).

115427.explanation: Residents should be able to co-decide on important matters. A local referendum strengthens involvement and also gives residents a say between elections.

115429.explanation: Secondary activities at farms are already possible and encourage entrepreneurship and business in the rural area.

115431.explanation: Housing construction has the priority. We have no space for distribution centers in our municipality!

115432.explanation: Heemskerk has no asylum center, so this cannot be closed.

115433.explanation: We accommodate genuine refugees, but not without caution, clarity, and support. For us, reception is only responsible if residents are involved in advance and concerns about safety and livability are taken seriously.

115435.explanation: We do not consider a fixed rule requiring a new tree to be automatically planted for every tree felled necessary. The municipality should make a wise assessment per situation without rigid replanting rules.

115436.explanation: Hart voor Heemskerk supports personal choice! The use of fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed. We do find it important that residents take responsibility and consider their surroundings. Therefore, education on responsible burning is important.

115438.explanation: If a nationwide fireworks ban is implemented, this is a nice way to celebrate New Year’s Eve together.

115442.explanation: We are not in favor of building entire residential neighborhoods in the rural area. Tailored solutions, such as a nursing home or a home for family members, can be negotiable.

115443.explanation: For children with low incomes, the sports and culture pass is the right instrument. We have it and it must continue to exist.

115444.explanation: Local broadcasters are important and we support them. Without subsidies, they cannot continue to exist.

115447.explanation: Sports clubs have great social value. If they run into trouble, the municipality must provide support so that sport remains accessible to everyone.

115449.explanation: More neighborhood-focused enforcement and additional enforcement officers ensure visibility, quick follow-up of reports, and safety in the neighborhood.

115450.explanation: In places where there is structural nuisance or insecurity, targeted camera surveillance can contribute to safety and enforcement.

115451.explanation: The need is great, but the scale must fit Heemskerk. It is important that appropriate reception is available, possibly through regional cooperation.

115452.explanation: The starter’s loan helps first-time buyers gain access to the housing market and must be retained.

115453.explanation: Residents of Heemskerk who have been waiting a long time for a home should not be unnecessarily disadvantaged.

115455.explanation: This can help alleviate the housing shortage, provided it is done carefully and does not cause nuisance.

115457.explanation: Hart voor Heemskerk stands for self-reliance. Those on welfare who can work should do so. Those on welfare who cannot work can do volunteer work and contribute to society in that way.

115461.explanation: Parking pressure is already high. Additional charging points must not come at the expense of regular parking spaces. Tailored solutions are needed.

115462.explanation: 30 km/h may be necessary in specific places, but a general reduction throughout the entire built-up area is not desirable. An assessment must be made per street.

115463.explanation: The municipality does not govern organization and freedom of choice in healthcare. Care must be accessible and appropriate, but this is not a municipal responsibility.

115465.explanation: Housing costs are already high. Hart voor Heemskerk submitted an amendment this year to lower the property tax (ozb), but unfortunately it did not pass. As a government, we must handle our residents’ money carefully.

115467.explanation: Tourism and recreation are important for the local economy and the tax was recently increased.

115473.explanation: Heemskerk currently does not have school swimming.

115476.explanation: Hart voor Heemskerk is not opposed to taller residential buildings if they provide extra homes. These buildings mainly belong in new residential areas or other suitable locations. In our village center densification is possible, but the village character must remain leading.

115478.explanation: Hart voor Heemskerk finds meeting places in neighborhoods important, but not every neighborhood needs its own community center. It is about activities and resident involvement, not about obligatorily erecting buildings.

115479.explanation: The center must remain easily accessible for residents and visitors. Hart voor Heemskerk believes parking in the center should remain free so that shops and hospitality remain attractive.

115480.explanation: Additional parking spaces are needed to keep the center accessible. In the long term, a central parking facility or garage can help, provided it is well integrated.

115482.explanation: Hart voor Heemskerk values a healthy living environment but is not in favor of additional bans. Initiatives that encourage smoke-free behavior work better than enforcement. Where smoke-free is desired, it can be voluntary.

statements.title: Playgrounds in Heemskerk should remain smoke-free.

titleParts.glossary: You may not smoke or vape here.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The residents of Heemskerk should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Heemskerk moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
Heemskerk should establish a refuge for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
More parking spaces should be created in the center.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
Heemskerk should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
There should be more CCTV surveillance in Heemskerk.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: More high-rise residential buildings should be allowed in Heemskerk.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: In Heemskerk, the use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In Heemskerk moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: Every neighborhood in the city should get its own community center.
The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to provide funding for school swimming lessons.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar blijven stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: Paid parking in the center should be made cheaper.
The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: See distribution center.
